LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TELEGRAPH LANDING NORTH ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, WHICH IS NOW MOOT, AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. NO EVIDENCE OF "SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS." MOTION DENIED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, NEGLIGENCE AND NUISANCE CLAIMS. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER THE ASSOCIATION BREACHED A DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS TO MAINTAIN THE COMMON AREAS. (UMF 9). DEFENDANT HIRED FIRMS TO DEAL WITH THE LEAK AND ASBESTOS ISSUES, AND THERE WERE DELAYS NOT ATTRIBUTABLE TO PLAINTIFF IN COMPLETING THE REPAIRS. WHETHER THE ASSOCIATION COULD HAVE REMEDIED THE PROBLEMS MORE QUICKLY IS A QUESTION OF FACT. THERE IS ALSO THE TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING DAMAGES, AND WHETHER FEES PAID ON A UNIT ARE RECOVERABLE. ORDERS RE: DEFT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE AND PLTF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE BOTH FILED IN OPEN COURT AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT TODAY'S HEARING. (THE COURT'S RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ISSUES BEING BROUGHT BEFORE THE TRIAL JUDGE.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TELEGRAPH LANDING NORTH ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Clyde I. Butts, Attorney at Law, (925) 943-1850 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Mark G. Intrieri, Esq. of Chapman & Intrieri, LLP (310) 540-7883 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication granted as to injunctive relief, which is now moot, and the cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. No evidence of "serious emotional distress." Motion denied as to cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, negligence and nuisance claims. Triable issue of fact raised whether the association breached a duty to plaintiffs to maintain the common areas. (UMF 9). Defendant hired firms to deal with the leak and asbestos issues, and there were delays not attributable to plaintiff in completing the repairs. Whether the Association could have remedied the problems more quickly is a question of fact. There is also the triable issue of fact regarding damages, and whether fees paid on a unit are recoverable. Orders re: Deft's objections to evidence and Plft's objections to Evidence both filed in Open Court and copies given to counsel at today's hearing. (The Court's ruling is without prejudice to issues being brought before the trial Judge.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353
